flashcards.front: What is the time complexity of Strassen's algorithm?
flashcards.back: O(n²·⁸¹) or O(n^log₂7) through divide-and-conquer approach

flashcards.front: How many matrix multiplications does Strassen's algorithm require for subproblems?
flashcards.back: 7 multiplications instead of 8 in standard method

flashcards.front: What matrix size typically triggers the base case in Strassen's implementation?
flashcards.back: When matrices reach 1x1 or small threshold size

flashcards.front: What mathematical operation replaces some multiplications in Strassen's method?
flashcards.back: Matrix addition/subtraction to reduce computation steps

flashcards.front: Why is Strassen's algorithm not always faster in practice?
flashcards.back: Higher constant factors and memory overhead for small matrices

flashcards.front: What paradigm does Strassen's algorithm primarily use?
How does Strassen handle matrix partitioning?
flashcards.back: Divides matrices into four n/2 x n/2 submatrices

flashcards.front: What key insight enabled Strassen's complexity improvement?
flashcards.back: Replacing 8 multiplications with 7 through clever linear combinations

flashcards.front: What is the space complexity of Strassen's algorithm?
flashcards.back: O(n²) due to recursive submatrix storage

flashcards.front: Name two applications of matrix multiplication algorithms
flashcards.back: Computer graphics transformations, neural network operations

flashcards.front: What mathematical concept underlies Strassen's efficiency?
How does Strassen's algorithm affect numerical stability?
flashcards.back: Introduces more floating-point errors due to added/subtracted terms
